role: A small presentational control component that lets the user switch among three view modes of the application.
capabilities: Render three view-mode toggle buttons (both, circle, square)
Show inline SVG glyphs representing each mode
Apply an active-state CSS class for the selected mode
Call a parent-supplied setView callback when a mode is clicked
Attach address stamps for UI instrumentation
substance_why: The component is fully implemented: it renders interactive buttons with labels, glyphs, click handlers, active-state styling, and instrumentation stamps, with no TODOs or stubs.
interactions_present: Click a mode button to change the active view
Visual active-state feedback via CSS class
Accessible name and tooltip via aria-label/title
interactions_missing: No explicit keyboard arrow-key navigation between toggle buttons
No disabled, loading, or read-only state
No visible text label independent of icon/title
reuse_why: The mode-switching concept and configuration are worth keeping, but the component should be rebuilt to separate icons, styling, and instrumentation concerns.
decomposition_hints: Extract the MODES configuration and SVG glyphs into a dedicated icons/config module
Separate the address-stamping concern into a wrapper or hook so the toggle itself is pure UI
Split the button rendering into a reusable ToggleButton or IconButton primitive
